question_title: Will any single film win at least five Academy Awards at the 98th Academy Awards?
summary: The 98th Academy Awards ceremony takes place March 15, 2026 (2 days away). Two films dominate nominations: Sinners (record 16 nominations) and One Battle After Another (13 nominations). Expert predictions from 5 major outlets (Variety, Deadline, IndieWire, The Ringer, Gold Derby) show 4/5 predicting at least one film will reach 5+ wins. Sinners has near-locks in Actor, Original Screenplay, Score, and Casting (4 categories), needing just 1 more. OBAA is favored in Picture, Director, Adapted Screenplay, and Editing. A Monte Carlo simulation with correlated outcomes yields ~69% probability. The main downside risk is a two-film split (historical precedent: 2020, 2019 ceremonies), but the sheer depth of nominations and near-lock categories makes 5+ wins likely for at least one film.

factors.description: 4 out of 5 major prediction outlets (Variety, Deadline, IndieWire, Gold Derby) predict at least one film winning 5+ awards. Variety predicts Sinners winning 7, others predict 5-6 for the leading film.
Historical base rate: 6/13 recent ceremonies (46%) had a film win 5+. This year's conditions (record 16 nominations for Sinners, 13 for OBAA) are above-average for dominance.

Sinners has near-lock wins in 4 categories (Actor ~85%, Original Screenplay ~80%, Score ~90%, Casting ~80%). Only needs 1 additional win from competitive categories (Supporting Actress, Sound, Cinematography) to reach 5.
OBAA is favored in Picture (~70%), Director (~55%), Adapted Screenplay (~85%), and Editing (~65%). With Supporting Actor (~50%) and Cinematography (~55%) as additional paths, it could also reach 5+.
Two-film split could prevent either from reaching 5. Historical parallels: 2020 (Parasite 4, 1917 3) and 2019 (Bohemian Rhapsody 4, Roma 3) where competitive splits kept both films below 5.
BAFTA precursor: OBAA won 6 BAFTAs (including Best Film), demonstrating sweep potential in craft categories that often predict Oscar outcomes.
Monte Carlo simulation with correlated category outcomes produces P(any film >=5) = 69%, consistent with expert consensus.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.70 (70% probability YES)**

Key reasoning:

- **Sinners** (16 nominations) has near-lock wins in 4 categories (Actor, Original Screenplay, Score, Casting) and only needs 1 more from competitive races to reach 5
- **One Battle After Another** (13 nominations) is favored in Picture, Director, Adapted Screenplay, and Editing — also a plausible path to 5+
- **4 out of 5** major expert outlets predict at least one film reaching 5+ wins
- **Monte Carlo simulation** with correlated outcomes gives ~69%
- The main risk is a two-film vote split keeping both below 5, but the depth of near-locks (especially for Sinners) makes this the less likely scenario
- **Historical base rate** is 46%, but this year's nomination profile is stronger than average

resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the official 2026 Oscars winners page at https://www.oscars.org/oscars/ceremonies/2026 shows any single film receiving five or more wins. Otherwise, it resolves to no.
fine_print: Use the Academy's official winners list. A film's total includes all categories awarded during the 98th ceremony. Candidate Sources: Academy Awards ceremony page at https://www.oscars.org/oscars/ceremonies/2026 ; Academy press releases linked from the same site.
background_info:
This forecasts whether one film will dominate the 98th Oscars with five or more wins.
